findings.evidence: New theorem: `apply_mem_map ... (hc : c ∈ A) : f c ∈ A.map f`; Mathlib precedents include `Submodule.mem_map_of_mem` at `Mathlib/Algebra/Module/Submodule/Map.lean:94`, `Subgroup.mem_map_of_mem` at `Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Map.lean:131`, and `Submonoid.mem_map_of_mem` at `Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Submonoid/Operations.lean:227`.
findings.fix: Rename it to `mem_map_of_mem` and update uses.
findings.issue: `apply_mem_map` names the operation/proof shape rather than the conclusion; Mathlib image APIs use `mem_map_of_mem` for this statement.

findings.evidence: New theorem: `map_comp ... : A.map (g.comp f) = (A.map f).map g`; Mathlib precedents include `Subalgebra.map_map` at `Mathlib/Algebra/Algebra/Subalgebra/Basic.lean:431`, `StarSubalgebra.map_map` at `Mathlib/Algebra/Star/Subalgebra.lean:247`, `Subgroup.map_map` at `Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Map.lean:147`, and `Submodule.map_comp` is specifically for the underlying `Submodule.map` API at `Mathlib/Algebra/Module/Submodule/Map.lean:104`, not bundled algebraic subobjects.
findings.fix: Rename to `map_map`, preferably with orientation `(A.map f).map g = A.map (g.comp f)` to match Mathlib.
findings.issue: `map_comp` is nonstandard for the subobject map-map composition lemma; adjacent Mathlib subobject APIs call this `map_map`.

summary: Two introduced theorem names diverge from standard Mathlib subobject/image naming and should be renamed before exposing this API.
